findings.evidence: grep hit `Integral.lean:55:lemma iteratedDerivWithin_Icc_eq_Ici`; the proof only builds `hcda` from `hf.contDiffOn.contDiffAt` and rewrites both sides with `iteratedDerivWithin_eq_iteratedDeriv`.
findings.fix: State a general lemma from `ContDiffAt ℝ (n : WithTop ℕ∞) f t` (or a `ContDiffOn` hypothesis) and derive the CM wrapper from `hf.contDiffOn` if desired.
findings.issue: `IsCompletelyMonotone.iteratedDerivWithin_Icc_eq_Ici` assumes complete monotonicity, but only local smoothness at `t` is used.

findings.evidence: grep hit `Integral.lean:95:lemma integral_neg_deriv`; proof uses `hf.contDiffOn.mono ...` to call Mathlib `intervalIntegral.integral_derivWithin_Icc_of_contDiffOn_Icc`, with no sign or higher CM condition.
findings.fix: Prove the general `ContDiffOn` FTC form first, e.g. from `ContDiffOn ℝ 1 f (Ici 0)` plus `0 ≤ x ≤ T`, and keep the CM versions as wrappers if useful.
findings.issue: `IsCompletelyMonotone.integral_neg_deriv` and its `integral_mass` corollary assume complete monotonicity, but the FTC identity uses only `ContDiffOn ℝ 1 f` on the interval.

findings.evidence: grep hit `BernsteinMeasures.lean:74:lemma continuousOn_chafaiDensity`; after unfolding the density, the proof only calls `hcm.contDiffOn.continuousOn_iteratedDerivWithin`.
findings.fix: Generalize the lemma to take `ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ f (Ici 0)` and derive the current CM specialization from `hcm.contDiffOn`.
findings.issue: `continuousOn_chafaiDensity` assumes `IsCompletelyMonotone f`, but continuity of the density only needs smoothness of `f` on `[0, ∞)`.

summary: Some exported lemmas are still stated at the completely-monotone level even though their proofs only use smoothness, so the API is less reusable than the natural Mathlib level.
